items.title: Minimal modern pyproject.toml
items.scenario: You're starting `ragapp` from scratch — pure-Python, single package, no native code.
items.explanation: About 25 lines covers it: `[build-system]` with setuptools, `[project]` with name/version/deps, `[tool.setuptools.packages.find] where = ['src']`. `pip install -e .` and you're shipping-ready.

items.title: Add a CLI entry point
items.scenario: You want `ragapp index ./docs/` to work after `pip install ragapp`.
items.explanation: Add `[project.scripts] ragapp = "ragapp.cli:main"`. Installer creates a wrapper script on PATH that calls `ragapp.cli.main()`. Works on any OS.

items.title: Optional extras for dev tooling
items.scenario: You want `pip install ragapp[dev]` to grab pytest, mypy, ruff for contributors.
items.explanation: Add `[project.optional-dependencies] dev = ['pytest', 'mypy', 'ruff']`. Production installs stay lean; dev installs get everything in one command.

items.title: Switch from setuptools to hatchling
items.scenario: You want simpler config and faster, hermetic builds.
items.explanation: Change `[build-system]` to `requires = ['hatchling'], build-backend = 'hatchling.build'`. Replace `[tool.setuptools.packages.find]` with `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ['src/ragapp']`. `[project]` and `[tool.pytest/mypy/ruff]` are unchanged.

items.title: Read version from `__init__.py`
items.scenario: You don't want to maintain version in two places.
items.explanation: Use `dynamic = ['version']` and a backend mechanism (setuptools: `[tool.setuptools.dynamic] version = { attr = 'ragapp.__version__' }`; hatch: `[tool.hatch.version] path = 'src/ragapp/__init__.py'`). Single source of truth.
